reforms.description: The Ministry of Economic Affairs has prepared two special venture capital funds, the Start-up Fund and the Growth Fund, to support financing for start-ups and fast-growing companies in the ICT sector, along with thematic incubators providing essential support.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These funds are designed to enhance access to capital for innovative firms, thereby fostering entrepreneurship and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Ministry of Economic Affairs has prepared two special venture capital funds.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of these funds represents a significant effort to bolster the startup ecosystem, particularly in the ICT sector, which is crucial for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The Ministry of Economic Affairs has prepared two special venture capital funds (the Start-up Fund and the Growth Fund) to meet the financing requirements of start-ups and fast-growing companies in the information and communication technology sector (ICT).

reforms.description: The government is allocating over €130 million to demand-driven and innovation-oriented fundamental research, alongside €50 million for new Innovation-Oriented Research Programmes (IPOs), enhancing the research infrastructure and autonomy of publicly-funded research institutes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This funding aims to strengthen the research capacity and innovation potential of public research institutes, which is essential for long-term economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: A little over €130 million of public money will be allocated in the 2000-10 period to demand-driven and innovation-oriented fundamental research.
reforms.importance_rationale: This funding initiative represents a significant commitment to enhancing the research landscape and fostering innovation, which is vital for economic competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: A little over €130 million of public money will be allocated in the 2000-10 period to demand-driven and innovation-oriented fundamental research, and some €50 million will be allocated to the new Innovation-Oriented Research Programmes (IPOs).

reforms.description: The government plans to accelerate the liberalisation process in the electricity sector to enhance competition and efficiency, which is expected to lower prices and stimulate innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased competition in the electricity sector is likely to drive down prices and encourage investment in innovative technologies.
reforms.status_evidence: The government should confirm its intention of accelerating the liberalisation process in the electricity sector.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift towards a more competitive energy market, which is crucial for fostering innovation and improving economic efficiency.
reforms.source_quote: Hence, the government should confirm its intention of accelerating the liberalisation process in the electricity sector.

reforms.description: The government aims to dismantle barriers to entry for innovative entrepreneurs, including reforming the bankruptcy law and improving access to research facilities and venture capital.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing barriers for startups, the reform is expected to enhance entrepreneurial activity and innovation, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Other barriers to entry should also be dismantled, for instance by reforming the bankruptcy law, and reducing impediments for innovative entrepreneurs.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is a comprehensive approach to fostering a more conducive environment for startups, which is essential for long-term economic growth and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Other barriers to entry should also be dismantled, for instance by reforming the bankruptcy law, and reducing impediments for innovative entrepreneurs.

reforms.description: The government is preparing legislation to further develop the market for venture capital, aiming to enhance the emergence of new, innovative enterprises.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing venture capital availability is expected to stimulate the creation and growth of innovative firms, thereby boosting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is preparing legislation to further develop the market for venture capital.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it targets the venture capital market, which is crucial for supporting innovative startups, but it is not a comprehensive overhaul of the innovation system.
reforms.source_quote: The government aims to further develop the market for venture capital to enhance the emergence of new, innovative enterprises.

reforms.description: The Dutch government is phasing out the Establishment Law to reduce barriers to entrepreneurship, aiming for complete abolition by January 2006.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing regulatory barriers, the reform is expected to enhance entrepreneurship and stimulate the creation of new businesses, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The law will be entirely abolished by January 2006, at the latest.
reforms.importance_rationale: The complete abolition of the Establishment Law represents a significant structural change aimed at fostering a more dynamic entrepreneurial environment, which is crucial for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The scope of the Establishment Law was substantially reduced at the beginning of this year, and the Law will be entirely abolished by January 2006, at the latest.

reforms.description: The Dutch government intends to increase its investment to strengthen the Netherlands’ innovative capacity to a total of around €½ billion over the next decade, aiming to improve the transfer of knowledge to SMEs and support incubator facilities for high-tech and innovative start-ups from universities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing innovative capacity and supporting startups can lead to increased productivity and job creation in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government intends to increase its investment to strengthen innovative capacity.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing the innovation ecosystem in the Netherlands, with potential long-term impacts on economic growth and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The government intends to increase its investment to strengthen the Netherlands’ innovative capacity to a total of around €½ billion over the next decade.

reforms.description: The government has taken steps to improve the match between public research infrastructure and society’s knowledge requirements through the introduction of target funding from the Netherlands Organisation for Applied Scientific Research (TNO) and the Large Technological Institutes (GTIs).
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Aligning research infrastructure with societal needs can enhance the effectiveness of R&D investments and drive innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has already taken the initiative to improve the match between this infrastructure and society’s knowledge requirements.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to enhance the alignment of research efforts with societal needs, which is crucial for effective innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The government has already taken the initiative to improve the match between this infrastructure and society’s knowledge requirements.

reforms.description: The SME Credit Guarantee Scheme (BBMKB) aims to provide guarantees to banks that lend to new, small, and medium-sized businesses facing difficulties in accessing credit due to inadequate collateral.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating access to credit for SMEs, the scheme supports their growth and innovation potential, which can enhance overall economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme is operational and providing guarantees to banks.
reforms.importance_rationale: The scheme represents a significant effort to improve financing conditions for SMEs, which are crucial for innovation and economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: The SME Credit Guarantee Scheme (BBMKB) aims at giving guarantees to banks which make loans to new, small and medium-sized businesses with difficult access to bank credit under normal banking conditions, due to a lack of adequate collateral.

reforms.description: The cluster projects initiative aims to stimulate partnerships between innovative firms and publicly-financed research institutes, addressing systemic failures in knowledge interaction.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering collaboration between firms and research institutes, the initiative enhances knowledge transfer and innovation, which can lead to improved competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: The cluster projects have been playing an important role for several years in Dutch industrial policy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant approach to enhancing collaboration between the private and public sectors, which is essential for driving innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The so-called 'cluster projects' have been playing an important role for already several years in Dutch industrial policy and in efforts to stimulate partnerships between innovative firms and publicly-financed research institutes.
